items.headline: U.S. Futures Show Modest Gains Ahead of Key Inflation Data; Geopolitical Tensions Remain a Headwind
items.summary: U.S. stock futures are trending modestly higher this Friday morning, hinting at a potential stabilization after a sharp downturn on Thursday. However, overall market sentiment remains cautious as investors await crucial U.S. inflation data and Q4 GDP revisions. The ongoing conflict in Iran and persistently elevated crude oil prices continue to fuel stagflation fears, impacting risk appetite across global markets. Brent crude remains above $100 a barrel, with West Texas Intermediate around $95.3 per barrel.

items.statContext: Modestly higher in pre-market trading, following a 1.52% decline on Thursday to 6,672.58.

items.headline: Key U.S. Economic Data Expected Today: PCE and Q4 GDP Revision
items.summary: Investors are closely watching today's release of the Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) report, a key inflation gauge for the Federal Reserve. Consensus forecasts project headline PCE to rise 0.3% month-over-month and 2.9% year-over-year. The first revision of Q4 U.S. GDP growth and the preliminary March Consumer Sentiment index are also scheduled for release.

items.headline: Butler National Corporation Reports Strong Q3 FY2026 Results
items.summary: Butler National Corporation ($BNL) announced third-quarter fiscal year 2026 financial results today, with revenue increasing 27% to $26.9 million. Operating income surged 129% to $9.2 million, and net income rose 98% to $6.7 million, translating to earnings per share of $0.10.

items.headline: VEON Ltd. Delivers Strong Q4 and Full Year 2025 Financial Results
items.summary: VEON Ltd. ($VEON), a global digital operator, reported robust financial and operating results for Q4 and full year ended December 31, 2025, today. Fourth-quarter total revenue grew 17.4% year-on-year to $1,171 million, and EBITDA increased 29.1% year-on-year to $527 million.

items.headline: Iran Conflict and Oil Prices Continue to Weigh on Market Sentiment
items.summary: The ongoing geopolitical tensions surrounding the Iran conflict are a primary driver of market caution. Surging crude oil prices, with Brent remaining above $100 per barrel, are contributing to fears of stagflation and have led traders to reconsider expectations for Federal Reserve rate cuts in 2026.

items.headline: Industrials, Consumer Discretionary, and Health Sectors Lagged Yesterday
items.summary: On Thursday, eight of the eleven S&P sectors concluded the session in negative territory, with Industrials, Consumer Discretionary, and Health sectors experiencing the most significant declines. The surge in oil prices particularly affected airline stocks.
